title: Column-Family Stores Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about column-family stores and their role in modern web applications.

questions.question: What is a 'Column-Family Store' (Wide-Column) and why is it useful?
options: A way to backup the database.
A NoSQL database that stores data in columns rather than rows, providing incredible write performance and efficiency.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: Which of these is the most popular open-source column-family store, famously used by Netflix and Apple?
What's the meaning of 'Append-only storage' in a columnar database?
options: Data is always written at the end of the file, never overwriting existing data, providing massive write throughput.

questions.question: Which of these is a typical use case for a column-family store?
options: Simple prototypes where the schema changes daily.
Storing and querying time-series data or massive IoT sensor logs.

questions.question: Why is it generally better to 'Denormalize' data in a column-family store?
options: Columnar databases are not designed for JOINs; duplicating data to support different query types is much faster.
To hide the application's IP address.
